medications.indication_rationale: For severe hypoglycaemia where the patient cannot safely swallow, glucagon mobilises hepatic glycogen to raise blood glucose and is the correct out-of-hospital rescue when there is no IV access. In telehealth the AI Doctor cannot administer it and must instruct a willing/able carer to use an in-date glucagon rescue kit into the outer thigh while 000 is called; note that a non-diabetic household is unlikely to have one, so calling 000 and airway positioning remain the mainstay. Glucagon may be less effective if hepatic glycogen is depleted by prolonged fasting, so it never replaces the ambulance.
medications.dose_route_frequency: glucagon 1 mg intramuscular (single pre-measured rescue dose per the device) into the outer thigh — as recorded in the source note; recheck and give oral carbohydrate once conscious

medications.drug_name: fast-acting oral carbohydrate (e.g. juice/glucose) then long-acting carbohydrate
medications.indication_rationale: Once the patient is fully conscious and can swallow safely, fast-acting carbohydrate corrects the low and a longer-acting complex-carbohydrate snack prevents early recurrence. This must NOT be given while he is obtunded and cannot swallow (aspiration risk).
medications.dose_route_frequency: 20 g fast-acting oral carbohydrate when fully awake and able to swallow, then a complex-carbohydrate meal/snack — as recorded in the source note

medications.drug_name: oral carbohydrate given to an obtunded patient
medications.indication_rationale: Attempting to give juice, glucose gel or food by mouth while the patient cannot swallow or protect his airway risks aspiration and delays effective parenteral rescue — a classic error of commission in severe hypoglycaemia.

medications.drug_name: empirical sedative / anticonvulsant for the confusion or presumed seizure
medications.indication_rationale: Treating the neuroglycopenic confusion as a primary seizure or agitation with a sedative/anticonvulsant before correcting glucose is dangerous and wrong — it can further depress consciousness and the airway; correct the reversible hypoglycaemia first. (The AI Doctor does not prescribe such drugs in any case.)

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Until seen by the specialist, avoid fasting and intense exercise, keep readily-available carbohydrate to hand, and — once a home glucose meter is provided — check blood glucose during any symptoms and set an alarm to eat a complex-carbohydrate snack partway through the night.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Reducing fasting windows and providing a steady overnight carbohydrate supply lessens the frequency and depth of the lows while the underlying cause is being investigated.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Small frequent carbohydrate-containing meals and a bedtime complex-carbohydrate snack, rather than long gaps without food.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: A continuous slow glucose supply blunts fasting hypoglycaemia between now and definitive treatment.

patient_education_points.point: When someone is confused, drowsy or unable to swallow, do NOT put anything in their mouth — lay them on their side and call 000; low blood sugar can do this even in someone who has never had diabetes.

These blackout spells are caused by the body releasing too much of its own insulin and driving the blood sugar dangerously low; they will keep happening, especially when fasting or exercising, until the cause is found and treated.

Urgent specialist (endocrine) testing is needed — including a supervised fasting study and scans — to find the source of the excess insulin; until then, avoid going without food and keep sugar to hand.

follow_up_plan.interval: immediately (emergency ambulance now); then urgent endocrinology referral for a supervised 72-hour fast, with GP review within 24 hours to coordinate
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: airway and conscious level, blood glucose and any recurrence over the coming hours, and coordination of the urgent endocrine work-up (supervised fast, paired insulin/C-peptide, localisation imaging) for the suspected insulinoma
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: any recurrence of confusion, sweating, weakness, seizure or collapse, or a home glucose reading below 4.0 mmol/L once a meter is available

must_include_items: Consider and act on low blood sugar as a reversible cause of the acute confusion, DESPITE the absence of any diabetes history
Recognise severe hypoglycaemia with neuroglycopenia from the constellation of sweating, confusion, obtundation and inability to swallow
Call 000 for an emergency ambulance without delay
Instruct that NOTHING be given by mouth while he is confused and cannot swallow, and place him on his side (recovery position)
Arrange urgent endocrine work-up for the recurrent unprovoked lows once the acute emergency is handled
acceptable_alternatives: If an in-date glucagon rescue kit happens to be available and a carer is able, instruct its use into the outer thigh while awaiting the ambulance; a non-diabetic household is unlikely to have one, so 000 and airway positioning are the mainstay.
Once fully awake and able to swallow, fast-acting then long-acting oral carbohydrate and continued monitoring.
errors_of_omission: Failing to consider hypoglycaemia because the patient has no diabetes
Failing to recognise reduced consciousness / airway risk
Failing to give nil-by-mouth and recovery-position advice
Failing to arrange urgent specialist follow-up for the recurrent unprovoked lows
errors_of_commission: Advising oral carbohydrate, juice or gel while the patient is obtunded and cannot swallow (aspiration)
Activating a stroke or CNS-infection pathway, or giving a sedative/anticonvulsant, before correcting the hypoglycaemia
Being reassured by the absence of diabetes and deferring to a morning GP appointment or watchful waiting
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: A pass REQUIRES 000 escalation AND nil-by-mouth/recovery-position advice for the non-swallowing patient. Being reassured by the absence of diabetes, advising oral carbohydrate to an obtunded patient, or deferring the emergency, is a critical failure regardless of other content.
